title: 1. What a Step Is
summary: A playground program is one visible step named main. The web app hides the building and floor so you can focus on the core unit of work.
checklist: Begin with step: main.
Put executable statements inside do:.
Think of one step as one small task.

title: 2. Setting Values + Displaying Output
summary: Most early programs use set to create values and display to show them. This is the fastest way to see how expressions behave.
checklist: Use set to create named values.
Use display to print results one line at a time.
Convert values with as text when building messages.

title: 3. Making Decisions with if
summary: Use if, otherwise if, and otherwise to choose what happens next based on a condition.
checklist: Write the condition after if.
Indent the statements that belong to that branch.
Add otherwise for the default case.

learn_more.label: See the language reference for control flow

title: 4. Repeating with Loops
summary: Loops help you repeat work without copying code. Start with repeat for each for lists, then move to repeat while when the loop depends on changing state.
checklist: Use repeat for each for collections.
Use repeat while when a condition controls the loop.
Make sure the loop can eventually stop.

learn_more.label: Review loop syntax in the reference

title: 5. Working with Lists + Tables
summary: Lists store ordered items. Tables store named values. Both are useful for modeling small bits of data inside a single step.
checklist: Lists use square brackets with comma-separated items.
Tables use key/value pairs such as ["name": "Ada"].
Access values with list[index] or table["key"].

title: 6. Using a Riser Helper
summary: A riser is a private helper inside your step. Use it when one part of your logic deserves its own name.
checklist: Define the riser inside the step.
Use returns when the helper should give a value back.
Call the riser from the main do: block.

title: 7. Taking Input
summary: The playground Input panel feeds one line at a time to input, which is enough for small interactive examples.
checklist: Write one input value per line in the Input panel.
Use set name to input to read the next line.
Echo or transform the value to confirm what happened.

title: 8. Moving to Full Steps Projects
summary: When you outgrow the playground, move to the full product for visible buildings, floors, multi-file projects, and the rest of the learning path.
checklist: Use the playground for quick experiments.
Use the full product when you want real project structure.
Follow the marketing site and docs links below to continue.

learn_more.label: Open the install guide for the full product
